_readme: Apex monitor grid — panes are config, not code. This SAMPLE loads only when no panes.json exists beside it: copy it to panes.json and make the panes yours. A pane = an id, a title, a source, and widgets. Source types: 'system' (this machine's stats — CPU/memory/disk/uptime via Node's own os/fs APIs, zero permissions, nothing leaves the box), 'weather' (Open-Meteo, no API key; set source.city — omit it and the tile guesses from your IP, which lands on your ISP's region), 'demo' (fake wandering data, the template pane below), 'http-json' (poll a JSON endpoint: set source.base + source.pane; the shell GETs base/pane and binds the returned fields), or add your own module beside sourceDemo.js (one file: start(pane, ctx) -> stop, action(pane, id, ctx)). Widget kinds: led (good/warning/critical), stat (text/number), gauge (0..max fill), list (rows of name+value), tiles (a row of day-cards: name/icon/cond/hi/lo/rain — full view only), input (a free-text setting: posts cfg:<key>:<text> to the source, which validates/applies/persists — the weather Location field), buttons (fire 'action' verbs at your source), log (activity lines). 'bind' names the field of your source's emitted data each widget reads. Optional "only": "quarter" or "full" on a PANE keeps it out of the other view entirely (its bar chip stays), and on a WIDGET targets it per view (the quarter grid already shows only led/gauge/stat); "wide": true on a pane spans it across the full grid's width. The tracker-bar chip shows each pane's first led + first gauge/stat. No credentials in this file, ever.

panes.title: Storage
panes.service: OS drive — set source.disk for another
